questions: Explain the Node.js event loop and how it enables non-blocking I/O operations.
Describe the different ways to handle asynchronous operations in Node.js (callbacks, Promises, async/await).
answers: The event loop is single-threaded and handles async execution. It uses phases like timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, etc.
Callbacks are old. Promises use then/catch. Async/await is syntactic sugar over promises that looks clean.

evaluations.suggestions: Great answer on event loop phases. Mention libuv to show advanced knowledge.
Very clear summary of asynchronous options in Node.

strengths: Demonstrates a solid foundational understanding of the Node.js event loop and its various phases.
Clearly articulates the different asynchronous handling mechanisms in Node.js, from callbacks to async/await.
Exhibits good technical knowledge and confidence when discussing core Node.js concepts.
weaknesses: Could enhance explanations by incorporating deeper context, such as mentioning underlying libraries like libuv.
Opportunities exist to provide more nuanced discussions on foundational concepts, including the historical context or specific use cases of callbacks.
There is room to improve communication depth and detail when elaborating on complex technical topics.
